nodes.label: LiteStar Gateway Code Review Findings
nodes.rationale: Four review rounds of src/litestar_gateway (~6,300 LOC hexagonal architecture), each finding re-verified against source with file:line. Round 4 (2026-07-06): 0 CRITICAL, 1 HIGH, 7 MEDIUM, 8 LOW, concentrated on the edges of the new money machinery (reservation, settlement shield, streaming billing).

nodes.label: Pre-Call Budget Gate (_enforce_budget)
nodes.rationale: All six inference paths route through _prepare -> _enforce_budget; verified that no checkpoint exists between in_flight.total() and in_flight.add(), so concurrent requests cannot both slip under the limit on one replica.

nodes.label: Usage Outbox and Reconciler
nodes.rationale: Dead-letter for failed ledger writes, not a write-ahead intent: at-most-once on crash (documented per L13). Reconcile is idempotent check-insert-delete per row; runs unguarded on every replica because idempotency makes the lock intentionally optional (L14).

nodes.label: In-Flight Budget Reservation (InFlightSpend)
nodes.rationale: M24 shipped deliberately as the simpler option per the owner's call: an in-memory per-replica reservation (prompt estimate + max-tokens ceiling) instead of a durable reservation table, with the honest residual overshoot bound documented in docs/usage-cost.md.

nodes.label: Streaming Settlement (_metered_stream)
nodes.rationale: The single most complex function in the codebase: usage capture, estimation fallback, reservation release, shielded settlement, error-vs-ok trace branching, and billing in one generator. M26, M27, M29, and M30 all live in this file — the defect density is itself the signal (M32).

nodes.label: Hexagonal Architecture Boundaries (Verified Clean)
nodes.rationale: Grep-verified in every round: domain/ imports stdlib + pwdlib/anyio only; application/ has zero infrastructure/sqlalchemy/litestar imports; ports coherent; thin repos with service-layer tenant scoping accepted as a valid hexagonal split.

nodes.label: C1: Streaming Inference Unbilled and Untraced
nodes.rationale: Round 1's only CRITICAL: stream:true requests returned the provider iterator directly with no _observe call — zero billing, zero observability. Fixed in #51 by accumulating usage while iterating (include_usage) and recording at stream end.

nodes.label: H13: Client Disconnect Cancels Billing Write
nodes.rationale: CancelledError delivered at the provider await sailed through except Exception guards, so the finally's settlement ran inside an already-cancelled scope: no ledger row, no outbox row, no log, no trace. Exploitable free inference (drop connection before final chunk). Fixed in #91 with anyio.CancelScope(shield=True) around settlement.

nodes.label: H14: Vertex Embeddings Billed as Zero
nodes.rationale: from_gemini_embeddings hardcodes usage as None and _parse_usage coerces int(None or 0)=0; the embeddings path has no estimation fallback, so arbitrarily large Vertex embedding batches are free and invisible to the budget gate. Open as of Round 4.

nodes.label: M21: Budget Gate Blind to Outbox Spend
nodes.rationale: spend_since summed only usage_event, so cost dead-lettered to pending_usage_event was invisible to budget checks — a silent cap bypass exactly during the incident the outbox was built to survive. Fixed in #94 by counting outbox spend in the gate.

nodes.label: M26: Upfront Stream Rejection Bills Estimated Prompt
nodes.rationale: A provider 429/401/400 raised before any chunk still bills the estimated prompt because error is not None — charging the team for a request the provider rejected for free. The over-billing mirror of the deliberate L11/#103 under-billing fix, introduced by that fix and untested. Open.

nodes.label: M27: Reservation Leaks on Never-Iterated Stream Generator
nodes.rationale: Python never runs the finally of an async generator that is never started, so if Litestar never begins the SSE body the reservation leaks into per-replica InFlightSpend forever — accumulating until every request gets a false 402 until restart. Revenue-safe but a slow availability failure. Open, needs a design decision.

nodes.label: M28: Quarantined Outbox Rows Inflate the Budget Gate
nodes.rationale: The M21 and M23 fixes interact: a poisoned row quarantines after ~10 cycles but keeps counting as live spend in every budget check until the window rolls over — phantom spend shrinking usable budget. Undocumented, untested tradeoff. Open.

nodes.label: M29: Settlement Shield Has No Timeout
nodes.rationale: The H13 shield is correct but unbounded: nothing can cancel it and nothing times out, so under DB degradation every disconnecting stream leaves an immortal cleanup coroutine holding a pool connection. Fix: anyio.move_on_after falling through to the outbox dead-letter path. Open, needs a design decision.

nodes.label: M30: Reservation Ignores n (up to 8x Understated)
nodes.rationale: _reservation_cost had no n multiplier while n is client-controllable up to MAX_N=8, so the real committed cost could be ~8x the reservation and the documented overshoot bound was understated. Fixed in #108: reservation scales with n.

nodes.label: M31: OIDC redirect_uri Falls Back to Unauthenticated Host Header
nodes.rationale: With OIDC_REDIRECT_URI unset, request.base_url derives from the raw Host header, letting a forged Host influence the declared redirect_uri; exploitability depends on the IdP's redirect-URI validation but the gateway offers no defense of its own. Open.

nodes.label: M32: Extract UsageMeter from CompletionService
nodes.rationale: completion_service.py fused five concerns in 576 lines; split the metering/billing collaborator out while the code is calm, before v2 routing lands on the hot spot. Fixed in #106.

nodes.label: L23: Reservation Computed from Pre-Sanitize Request
nodes.rationale: A max_tokens: 999999999 stream reserved an enormous phantom cost (self-DoS of the team's own gate) because sanitize_request clamped to 32k only after _enforce_budget ran. Fixed in #108: clamp first, reserve second.

nodes.label: M25: Sustainable Login Lockout DoS
nodes.rationale: 20 requests/hour kept a victim's password login locked forever after each 15-minute expiry. Fixed in #96 as exponential lockout with decay plus a platform-admin unlock endpoint, per the owner's direction.

nodes.label: H9: Insecure Default forwarded-allow-ips
nodes.rationale: The shipped entrypoint trusted X-Forwarded-For from any peer, letting a direct client forge a fresh rate-limit bucket per request (complete auth rate-limit bypass) and falsify audit IPs. Fixed in #71: defaults to loopback, opt-in via FORWARDED_ALLOW_IPS.

nodes.label: Credentials as a Global Pool (By Design)
nodes.rationale: Product decision: provider credentials are managed centrally by cloud ops (platform-admin only) and all orgs draw from one pool; secrets are never returned and api_base is credential-fixed. Round 2 recommends elevating optional org-scoping to required before selling multi-org, because cross-tenant spend blast radius is otherwise unbounded.

nodes.label: Per-Model max_output_tokens Proposal
nodes.rationale: Follow-up from the M30/L23 discussion: an optional per-model max_output_tokens as both the reservation fallback and a per-model clamp replacing the global 32k default. Prior art: LiteLLM's budget_reservation.py reserves min(requested, model max, 16384) scaled by n/best_of; its MIT registry could seed the field. Product decision — needs a migration and config surface.

edges.source_location: H13 impact: partially reopens round-1 C1
Round 4 M28: the M21 and M23 fixes interact
Round 4 M29: the H13 shield is correct but unbounded
